rawtext.text:
Ah! A customer! Or a bodyguard! Honestly, at this point I'll take either.

Orin Fairwind, purveyor of everything a besieged village forgets it needs until it desperately needs it. I've run the hill roads for twenty years and I have never — §onever§r — seen them this quiet. Quiet roads mean someone's waiting on them.

My father traded on these roads, and his mother before him. Fairwinds have been supplying Elderfall since the first roof went up on the ruins.

Grandmother had a rule she beat into every one of us: §o"Sell them anything, but never take payment in memories."§r I used to think it was a joke about hagglers with sob stories. Then last month, at the crossroads, a man in a rusted helm offered me a chest of gold for — and I quote — §o"one summer afternoon, any one you can spare."§r

I declined. I ran the horses half to death getting here. And now I sell rope and lanterns to the last village standing between §ohim§r and everything I remember.

As it happens — §6Wares for the Road§r. My stock of §f10 leather§r and §f5 string§r vanished with my second wagon. Replace it and I'll pay in emeralds, fair and square, plus a discount I'll almost certainly regret.

And if Kestrel hasn't bent your ear already: my final caravan run is the only thing standing between this village and empty larders. If you're the sword walking beside it, I'll feel a great deal better about my life expectancy.
